campsites.name: Reykjavík Campsite (Laugardalur)
campsites.notes: Large, well-equipped city campsite. Book ahead in summer.

campsites.name: Hveragerði Campsite
campsites.notes: Geothermal town campsite. Walking distance to hot spring hikes.

campsites.name: Geysir Campsite
campsites.notes: Right next to the geysers. Basic but perfectly located. Book ahead.

campsites.name: Skaftafell Campsite
campsites.notes: Massive campsite in stunning glacier scenery. No kitchen - bring camp stove. Arrive by 2pm.

campsites.name: Vík Campsite
campsites.notes: Best-located campsite on south coast. Walk to restaurants and black sand beach.

campsites.name: Höfn Campsite
campsites.notes: Friendly town campsite. Walking distance to lobster restaurants.

campsites.name: Hamragarðar Campsite
campsites.notes: Small, quiet campsite near Seljalandsfoss. Basic facilities. Incredible evening light.

campsites.name: Kleifar-Mörk Campsite
campsites.notes: Hidden gem between Vík and Jökulsárlón. Small, quiet, cheap. Waterfall behind camp.

campsites.name: Snæfellsnes Peninsula Campsite
campsites.notes: West Iceland peninsula campsite. Great coastal views.

campsites.name: Thórsmörk Campsite
campsites.notes: Mountain valley campsite. 4WD required. Stunning hiking area.

campsites.name: Landmannalaugar Campsite
campsites.notes: Highland campsite with natural hot springs. 4WD required. Opens late June.

campsites.name: Akureyri Campsite
campsites.notes: Large city campsite. Good base for exploring North Iceland.

campsites.name: Mývatn Campsite (Bjarg)
campsites.notes: Near Lake Mývatn. Great for geothermal areas and hiking.

campsites.name: Egilsstaðir Campsite
campsites.notes: East Iceland hub. Good facilities and central location.

campsites.name: Kirkjubæjarklaustur Campsite
campsites.notes: Good stop between Vík and Skaftafell. Town has grocery store.
